Admission Date:  1973-01-02              Discharge Date:   2004-10-02

Date of Birth:  2008-04-22             Sex:   F

Service: CARDIOTHORACIC

Allergies:
Sulfa (Sulfonamides)

Attending:Kim
Chief Complaint:
Rare Chest tightness

Major Surgical or Invasive Procedure:
1973-01-02 Ascending Aorta Replacement (34 mm Gelweave) and Aortic
Valve Replacement (23mm CE pericardial tissue)

History of Present Illness:
73 y/o with rare chest tightness. Underwent follow-up
echocardiogram which revealed an increasing ascending aortic
anuerysm.

Past Medical History:
Bipolar Disorder, Chronic Urinary Tract Infections, s/p Bilaeral
Varicose vein stripping, s/p Cholecystectomy

Social History:
Retired. 15 cigs/d x 50 years (quit 01-22). Drinks ETOH few times
a month.

Family History:
Father with Aortic Aneurysm

Physical Exam:
Preop:
VS: 86 20 120/84 124/88 5'2" 175#
Skin: Unremarkable
HEENT: NC/AT, EOMI, PERRL
Neck: Supple, FROM, -JVD
Chest: CTAB -w/r/r
Heart: RRR, +murmur which radiates to carotids.
Abd: Soft, NT/ND +BS
Ext: Warm, well-perfused, 2+ pulses throughout, Bilat.
varicosities
Neuro: Grossly intact, MAE

Discharge:
VS: T 98.8  HR 72SR BP 109/58  RR 20  O2sat 92%RA
Gen: NAD
Neuro: A&Ox3, MAE, follows commands, nonfocal exam
Pulm: CTA bilat
CV: RRR S1-S2, sternum stable, incision with steri strips-CDI
Abdm: soft, NT/ND/NABS
Ext: warm, well perfused, 1+ pedal edema-bilat

Pertinent Results:
Echo 10-19: PRE-BYPASS: Left ventricular wall thicknesses and
cavity size are normal. Overall left ventricular systolic
function is mildly depressed. Right ventricular chamber size and
free wall motion are normal. The ascending aorta is markedly
dilated The aortic arch is mildly dilated. There are simple
atheroma in the aortic arch. The descending thoracic aorta is
moderately dilated. There are simple atheroma in the descending
thoracic aorta. There are three aortic valve leaflets. There is
no aortic valve stenosis. Moderate (2+) aortic regurgitation is
seen. The mitral valve leaflets are mildly thickened. Trivial
mitral regurgitation is seen. POST-BYPASS: A bioprosthesis is
well seated in the Aortic position. All 3 leaflets move well.
Trace central AI is seen. A mean gradient of 03-12 mm of Hg is
obtained.  An ascending aortic graft is seen. LV function
appears slightly improved and RV function is unchanged.
CXR 07-29: 1. No significant change in left pleural effusion,
which has a small loculated lateral component. 2. Interval
decrease in the right pleural effusion, and improved bibasilar
aeration. 3. Unusual orientation of sternotomy wires, with
slight rightward displacement of uppermost wire. Clinical
correlation is recommended to exclude dehiscence.

Brief Hospital Course:
Ms. Andersen was a same day admit and day of admission went
directly to the operating room where she underwent an aortic
valve and ascending aorta replacement. Please see operative note
for surgical details. She tolerated the procedure well and was
transferred to the CSRU for invasive monitoring in stable
condition. Later on op day she was weaned from sedation, awoke
neurologically intact, and was extubated. On post-op day one her
chest tubes were removed and she was transferred to the SDU.
Beta blockers and diuretics were initiated and she was gently
diuresed towards her pre-op weight. On post-op day two her
epicardial pacing wires were removed. She required additional O2
with aggressive pulmonary toilet until post-op day four.
Physical therapy worked with patient for strength and mobility.
She recovered well without post-op complications and was
discharged home with VNA services and the appropriate follow-up
appointments on post-op day seven.

Metoprolol 25"

Discharge Disposition:
Home With Service

Facility:
9497 Ruth Greens Apt. 578
Thompsonland, WA 73022 VNA

Discharge Diagnosis:
Ascending Aneurysm s/p Ascending Aorta Replacement and Aortic
Valve Replacement
PMH: Bipolar Disorder, Chronic Urinary Tract Infections, s/p
Bilaeral Varicose vein stripping, s/p Cholecystectomy

Discharge Condition:
Good

Discharge Instructions:
Patient may shower, no baths. No creams, lotions or ointments to
incisions. No driving for at least one month. No lifting more
than 10 lbs for at least 10 weeks from the date of surgery.
Monitor wounds for signs of infection. Please call with any
concerns or questions.

Durhamp Instructions:
Madonna Rehabilitation Specialty Hospital clinic in 2 weeks
Dr. [**Last Name (Prefixes) **] in 4 weeks
Dr. Webster in 06-28 weeks
Dr. Penny in 09-04 weeks
